allGaps.description: ChatGPT categorises Turso as a "project management tool" while Claude describes it as a "design collaboration platform." The two positions imply different competitor sets and different buyers.
allGaps.whatFileNeeds: Positioning section with explicit category and "not this" statements.

evidence.chatgptSays: Turso is a project management tool for engineering teams.
evidence.claudeSays: Turso is a design collaboration and prototyping platform.
evidence.siteContent: Turso — the single source of truth for your product team.

allGaps.description: ChatGPT says Turso is self-hosted, Claude says it's cloud-only. The site says both are available.

evidence.chatgptSays: Turso runs entirely on your own infrastructure, self-hosted.
evidence.claudeSays: Turso is a cloud-only SaaS with no self-hosting option.
evidence.siteContent: Deploy in the cloud or self-host. Your call.

allGaps.description: Neither model names Turso's actual differentiator — the audit layer that catches AI hallucinations before they ship. Both models describe it as "another dev tool."
allGaps.whatFileNeeds: Positioning: explicit comparisons vs 2–3 well-known alternatives.

evidence.chatgptSays: Turso is one of many dev tools in the space, similar to its peers.
evidence.claudeSays: Turso offers a standard set of developer-productivity features.
evidence.siteContent: Turso is the only tool that checks AI outputs against your design decisions.

allGaps.description: ChatGPT invents a feature Turso doesn't ship — claiming built-in video chat and AI meeting summaries that aren't on the product.
allGaps.whatFileNeeds: Add a NotThis section listing features the product deliberately does not ship.

evidence.chatgptSays: Turso includes built-in video chat, AI-generated meeting summaries, and a native calendar sync out of the box.
evidence.claudeSays: Turso is focused on structured work; video and calendar are handled via integrations.
evidence.siteContent: We focus on one thing. Integrations available via our API.

allGaps.description: ChatGPT says Turso is free, Claude says it's $99/month enterprise-only. The truth is a freemium model with a $29 pro tier.
allGaps.whatFileNeeds: Context.pricing with the real tier structure written in one sentence.

evidence.chatgptSays: It's a free tool with no paid tiers.
evidence.claudeSays: Pricing starts at $99 per user per month for teams.
evidence.siteContent: Free to start. Pro is $29/month. Team is $99/month.

product.name: Turso
product.description: Turso — the single source of truth for how AI models should describe this product.

headline.description: ChatGPT invents a feature Turso doesn't ship — claiming built-in video chat and AI meeting summaries that aren't on the product.
headline.chatgptSays: Turso includes built-in video chat, AI-generated meeting summaries, and a native calendar sync out of the box.
headline.claudeSays: Turso is focused on structured work; video and calendar are handled via integrations.
headline.siteContent: We focus on one thing. Integrations available via our API.
